name: Massachusetts Institute of Technology

requirements.tests: SAT 1520+ or ACT 34+ (test-optional but recommended). TOEFL 100+ / IELTS 7.5+ for non-native English.
requirements.differentiators: Olympiad medals (IMO, IPhO, IOI), original research, exceptional projects

summary: MIT admits ~4% of applicants. Successful international applicants typically combine top-decile testing, deep technical projects, and either Olympiad-level competition results or original research.

name: Stanford University

requirements.differentiators: Intellectual vitality, founder-mindset, sustained impact in 1–2 areas over 'well-rounded'

summary: Stanford rewards depth and intellectual vitality. Founders, researchers, and athletes with one extraordinary signal beat well-rounded profiles.

name: Harvard University

requirements.differentiators: National/international leadership, distinguished awards, unique personal narrative

summary: Harvard values demonstrated leadership at scale and a coherent narrative. International applicants need standout national-level distinctions.

name: Carnegie Mellon University

requirements.differentiators: Strong programming portfolio, contest results (USACO Gold+, ICPC, IOI), demonstrated CS depth

summary: CMU SCS is the toughest CS admit in the US. Competitive programming results and shipped technical projects matter more than well-rounded ECs.

name: UC Berkeley

requirements.differentiators: Strong PIQs, sustained EC depth, demonstrated initiative

summary: Berkeley uses 4 Personal Insight Questions instead of recs. EECS is hyper-competitive — depth and demonstrated CS impact carry the application.

name: University of Chicago

requirements.differentiators: Intellectual curiosity demonstrated through unconventional thinking and academic depth

summary: UChicago rewards unconventional intellectuals. The essays are the soul of the application.

name: Princeton University

requirements.differentiators: Senior thesis culture — independent research signal valued highly

summary: Princeton is need-blind for internationals and values research aptitude. The graded paper requirement is unique.

name: Yale University

requirements.differentiators: Residential college culture — applicants who'll thrive in collaborative communities

summary: Yale is need-blind for internationals. Strong fit signal: collaborative, community-oriented leaders.

name: University of Oxford

requirements.tests: Course-specific tests (MAT, PAT, TSA, LNAT). IELTS 7.5+.
requirements.differentiators: Subject-specific aptitude tests + interview performance dominate the decision

summary: Oxford admits by subject. The aptitude test + interview matter more than well-rounded ECs.

name: University of Cambridge

requirements.tests: STEP for Maths, ENGAA for Engineering, etc. IELTS 7.5+.
requirements.differentiators: Tripos system — extreme subject depth expected from day one

summary: Cambridge expects subject mastery from application day. STEP-level math problem-solving is the deepest filter.

name: Imperial College London

requirements.tests: MAT for Maths, occasional interview. IELTS 6.5–7+.
requirements.differentiators: STEM-only focus — pure subject excellence wins

summary: Imperial is a STEM-only university. Pure subject excellence and grade ceilings carry the application.

name: University College London

requirements.differentiators: Cosmopolitan, international focus; strong personal statement matters

summary: UCL is more internationally accessible than Oxbridge while remaining a Top-10 UK research university.

name: University of Toronto

requirements.differentiators: Engineering Science and CS supplemental apps are the bar — strong math and coding background

summary: U of T's CS and Engineering Science programs are extremely competitive; the supplemental application is decisive.

name: University of Waterloo

requirements.tests: AIF (Admission Information Form). Euclid / CCC contests count.
requirements.differentiators: Math contest results (Euclid, CCC) directly boost admission. Co-op program is unique.

summary: Waterloo CS is contest-driven: Euclid and CCC results materially shift admission probability.

name: National University of Singapore

requirements.essays: Personal statement + interview for some programs
requirements.differentiators: Strong international applicant pool; Asian-context activities valued

summary: NUS is Asia's top university for STEM. South Asian applicants compete with the strongest regional pool.

name: Nanyang Technological University

requirements.differentiators: Engineering-heavy focus, strong industry partnerships

summary: NTU is a strong, slightly more accessible alternative to NUS for STEM.

name: Technical University of Munich

requirements.tests: TestAS for some programs. IELTS 6.5+ or German B2.
requirements.differentiators: Low tuition for internationals (~€150/sem). Strong industry pipeline.

summary: TUM offers world-class STEM education at near-zero tuition — a huge ROI play for South Asian families.

name: IIT Bombay

requirements.differentiators: JEE Advanced rank is the single gatekeeping signal

summary: IIT Bombay is exam-only: JEE Advanced rank determines admission. No essays, no ECs.

name: Bangladesh University of Engineering and Technology

requirements.tests: BUET admission test (Math, Physics, Chem, English)
requirements.differentiators: Admission test rank is decisive. No essays or ECs considered.

summary: BUET is Bangladesh's top engineering school. Pure HSC + admission test performance.

name: Institute of Business Administration (DU)

requirements.differentiators: Admission test heavily weights English and analytical reasoning

summary: IBA-DU is Bangladesh's most prestigious business school. The admission test is the entire filter.
